findings: The hook frames factoring as the reverse of last module's expansion (Module 12), giving students an immediate mental hook before any pattern terminology shows up.

The overview slide names exactly four strategies in the order students should try them - GCF first, then trinomial pair-search, then the two special patterns - which matches how an experienced student actually decides what to do.
The concept slide makes the verification step (re-expand and check) explicit, so students learn that factoring has a built-in correctness check rather than treating it as a guess-and-pray skill.
The trinomial slide pairs the worked recipe for x^2 + 8 x + 15 with a sign-rules card on the right, so a student who freezes on a problem with negative b or c has a decision rule in view.

Both pause problems are solvable from material already shown, and each is followed by a visually distinct answer-reveal slide that shows the full algebra step by step rather than only the final answer.

The sum-of-squares trap slide directly targets the most common Algebra I factoring error - treating x^2 + 25 as a difference of squares - and the wrong / right columns highlight the sign as the deciding signal.

The closing path slide gives students concrete Learn Portal next actions (OpenStax reading, Khan Academy practice, dashboard assignment, advisor check-in) rather than generic encouragement, and previews Module 14 as the natural next step.
watch_points: Slide 6 (trinomial) packs the worked example and the sign rules onto one frame; narration must move slowly and contact-sheet thumbnail should be re-checked at small size.

Students who try the difference-of-squares pattern on a sum (x^2 + 25) and on non-square constants should be flagged for advisor follow-up before Module 14 - the zero-product property leans on confident factoring.
